setting.note: In eastern Algeria under French colonial rule, four young men, Rachid, Lakhdar, Mourad and Mustapha, circle around the beautiful, unattainable Nedjma, a woman of mixed blood who comes to stand for Algeria itself. Kateb Yacine's fractured, founding novel of Algerian literature maps the real east: the port and worksites of Bône, the gorge-cut city of Constantine, the ancestral lands of the Keblout tribe, and the colonial labour and violence out of which a national consciousness is being born.

rights.originalWork: In copyright. Kateb Yacine, first published 1956. Mapped as a cited work: no text stored, no passages reproduced. The places are facts and the notes are our own.
rights.textSource: None stored. A cited book reproduces no text.
rights.territoryNotes: Locations and original commentary only. No reproduction of the copyrighted text, in any territory.
rights.attribution: Nedjma, Kateb Yacine (1956). Locations mapped by Read the Places; not affiliated with or endorsed by the author's estate or publisher.

palette.note: The gold of a star, which is what the name Nedjma means, the star of an Algeria not yet born that the four men reach for and never hold. A cited book's colour is not a quotation, because we store no text to quote; it still has to be legible, and this clears WCAG AA on paper.
